source: Intro to Optimization in Cloud.pdf, Page 5, Slide 8
question: What is a key characteristic of Nonlinear optimization (NLP)?
options: It solves problems with curves, products, or powers.
It only uses linear equations for objectives.
It guarantees finding the best solution quickly.
correct_answers: It solves problems with curves, products, or powers.

source: Intro to Optimization in Cloud.pdf, Page 5, Slide 9
question: What is the primary difference between Convex and Nonconvex problems regarding minimums?
options: Convex has one minimum, Nonconvex has many.
Convex has many minimums, Nonconvex has one.
correct_answers: Convex has one minimum, Nonconvex has many.

source: Intro to Optimization in Cloud.pdf, Page 5, Slide 9
question: Why are non-convex problems often necessary for complex machine learning tasks despite being NP-hard?
options: A "good enough" local solution is acceptable.
They always find the global optimal point.
correct_answers: A "good enough" local solution is acceptable.

source: Intro to Optimization in Cloud.pdf, Page 5, Slides 8-9
question: Match the optimization term with its characteristic from the text.
options: Finds best outcome using linear equations.
Solves problems with curves, products, or powers.
Provide guarantees of finding the best solution quickly.
Are generally NP-hard and have many minimums.

correct_answers.A: Finds best outcome using linear equations.
correct_answers.B: Solves problems with curves, products, or powers.
correct_answers.C: Provide guarantees of finding the best solution quickly.
correct_answers.D: Are generally NP-hard and have many minimums.
